ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT THE ACTION WILL NECESSARILY RESULT IN A VERDICT LESS THAN $25,000. WALKER VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1991) 53 CAL.3D 257. COUNSEL MAY BRING STIPULATION AND PROPOSED ORDER REGARDING REDACTING PLAINTIFF'S SOCIAL SECURITY NUMBER FROM ANY DOCUMENTS THAT HAVE BEEN FILED. Off Calendar May-04-2010 Set For Trial On Jan-18-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Reclassify Action To The Court Of Limited Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jun-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SANDI YOUNG, Motion to Reclassify Action to the Court of Limited Jurisdiction. Appearances: David A. Lerman, Esq. Attorney at Law, (510) 665-8500 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Milan Yancich, Esq. of Philip M. Andersen & Associates (415) 267-3600 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. Insufficient showing that the action will necessarily result in a verdict less than $25,000. Walker vs. Superior Court (1991) 53 Cal.3d 257. Counsel may bring stipulation and proposed order regarding redacting Plaintiff's social security number from any documents that have been filed. (Counsel to specify which documents are to be redacted.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
